company_profile.description: 51Talk Online Education Group, through its subsidiaries, engages in the provision of online education platform with English language education services to students in the Asia-Pacific regions and internationally. It operates online and mobile education platforms that enable students to take live interactive English and Chinese lessons, on demand, fostering the development of all aspects of English and Chinese proficiency. The company's flagship courses include Classic English Junior and Classic English for the development of English communication skills, as well as AI-empowered knowledge preview and AI-empowered reading lessons. The company was formerly known as China Online Education Group and changed its name to 51Talk Online Education Group in September 2022. 51Talk Online Education Group was founded in 2011 and is headquartered in Singapore.

company_profile.type_rationale: Cyclical business (basic materials) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
company_profile.description: Contango Silver & Gold Inc. engages in the exploration, development, and production of gold and associated minerals in Alaska and the Golden Triangle in British Columbia. It primarily focuses on exploring for gold and silver deposits. The company was formerly known as Contango Ore, Inc. and changed its name to Contango Silver & Gold Inc. in March 2026. Contango Silver & Gold Inc. was founded in 2009 and is headquartered in Fairbanks, Alaska.
company_profile.description_summary:
Explores, develops, and produces gold and associated minerals across Alaska and British Columbia's Golden Triangle. The company generates revenue primarily from gold and silver extraction operations and exploration activities in these mineral-rich regions.

The company distinguishes itself through its strategic focus on the prolific Golden Triangle area, known for significant precious metal deposits, combined with operations in Alaska's established mining districts.

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (basic materials) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

company_profile.name: China Pharma Holdings, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: China Pharma Holdings, Inc. develops, manufactures, and markets pharmaceutical products for human use in the People's Republic of China. The company provides products in the form of dry powder injectables, liquid injectables, tablets, capsules, and cephalosporin oral solutions. Its products include Cerebroprotein Hydroloysate injection to treat memory decline and attention deficit; Gastrodin injection for tiredness, loss of concentration, poor sleep, and traumatic syndromes of the brain; Propylgallate injection to treat cerebral thrombosis, coronary heart disease, and after surgery complications; Ozagrel Sodium to treat acute thrombotic cerebral infarction and dyskinesia; Alginic Sodium Diester injection for ischemic heart, cerebrovascular, and high lipoprotein blood diseases; Bumetanide injection for edema diseases, hypertension, acute renal failure, hyperkalemia, hypercalcemia, and acute drug poisoning; and Candesartan for hypertension. It offers Roxithromycin dispersible tablets for pharyngitis and tonsillitis; Cefaclor dispersible tablets for tympanitis, lower respiratory tract infection, urinary tract infections, and skin/skin tissue infection; Cefalexin capsules for acute tonsillitis; Andrographolide for sore throat caused by upper respiratory tract infection; Clarithromycin granules and capsules; and Naproxen Sodium and Pseudophedrine Hydrochlorida sustained release tablets, as well as Hepatocyte growth-promoting factors, Tiopronin, Compound Ammonium Glycyrrhetate S, and Omeparzole for the treatment of digestive diseases. In addition, the company provides Vitamin B6 injection; Granisetron Hydrochloride injection for nausea and vomiting caused by radiotherapy and chemotherapy during the treatment of malignant tumors; Noni Enzyme, a food supplement; sanitizers; and masks. It supplies its products to hospitals and OTC pharmacies through provincial and municipal pharmaceutical logistics companies. The company is based in Haikou, the People's Republic of China.
company_profile.description_summary:
China Pharma Holdings develops, manufactures, and markets pharmaceutical products in China across multiple dosage forms. Its portfolio includes injectables for neurological and cardiovascular conditions, oral antibiotics and antiinflammatories, digestive disease treatments, and oncology supportive care products. The company distributes through pharmaceutical logistics networks to hospitals and retail pharmacies.

The company also produces vitamin supplements, sanitizers, and masks, diversifying beyond prescription pharmaceuticals into consumer health and wellness products.

company_profile.name: CEL-SCI Corporation
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: CEL-SCI Corporation, a clinical-stage biotechnology company, engages in the research and development to treat cancer and other diseases by using the immune system in the United States. The company's lead immunotherapy is Multikine, which has completed Phase III clinical trials for the potential treatment of certain head and neck cancers. It is also developing Ligand Epitope Antigen Presentation System (LEAPS) technology, a patented T-cell modulation process that stimulates the human immune system to fight bacterial, viral, parasitic infections, autoimmune conditions, allergies, transplantation rejections, and cancer, as well as to potentially treat rheumatoid arthritis. In addition, the company's LEAP product candidates include CEL-2000; CEL-4000; and CEL-5000 that are in preclinical trials for potential rheumatoid arthritis therapeutic treatments. CEL-SCI Corporation has a strategic partnership with Saudi Arabian Pharma Company for Multikine in the treatment of head and neck cancer. The company was incorporated in 1983 and is headquartered in Vienna, Virginia.
company_profile.description_summary:
This clinical-stage biotech company develops immunotherapies to treat cancer and infectious diseases by harnessing the immune system. Its lead candidate, Multikine, has completed Phase III trials for head and neck cancer treatment, and it maintains a strategic partnership with Saudi Arabian Pharma Company for this program.

The company's distinctive LEAPS technology platform modulates T-cells to combat various conditions including bacterial and viral infections, autoimmune diseases, allergies, and cancer. Multiple LEAP product candidates are in preclinical development for rheumatoid arthritis treatment.

metric_assessments.financial_strength: With a debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 0.74 and a current ratio of 1.03, Costco carries a healthy balance sheet typical of investment-grade companies and can service obligations comfortably. metric_assessments.growth: Revenue growth of 21.5% and expected earnings per share growth of 11.1% over the next five years both rank in the top tier for mature retailers, earning the 8/10 score. The ceiling on the score reflects that 11% earnings growth, while strong, is still moderate compared to technology or healthcare businesses, and as Costco matures further, this rate may slow.
metric_assessments.predictability: The 10/10 score reflects the membership business model, which generates recurring, highly visible revenue and makes earnings highly forecastable year to year. Members renew on predictable schedules, inventory turns at consistent rates, and same-store sales trends are visible quarters in advance—a level of business stability that is exceptional and rare among retailers.
metric_assessments.valuation: A 4/10 valuation score means the stock is trading at a premium to its intrinsic value, not a discount or fair price. _meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? metric_assessments.growth: Revenue growing at 13.3% annually and earnings forecast to expand 12.6% over the next five years represent healthy, mid-single-digit-plus growth, but this is typical or slightly below-average for a high-quality software company and signals the business is maturing. Slower growth makes the stock more dependent on valuation multiple compression or operational efficiency gains to deliver returns.
metric_assessments.predictability: Salesforce's subscription-based business model with sticky customers and a 7/10 predictability score reflects the visibility that comes from recurring annual contracts—investors can forecast revenue with reasonable confidence. The company's enterprise customer base and dominant market position in CRM create durable demand and steady cash flows that don't surprise analysts often. metric_assessments.valuation: A 7/10 valuation score suggests the stock is trading at a fair to slightly attractive price relative to fundamentals—not cheap, but not overextended either. company_profile.description: Credit Acceptance Corporation engages in the provision of financing programs, and related products and services in the United States. It advances money to automobile dealers in exchange for the right to service the underlying consumer loans; and buys the consumer loans from the dealers and keeps the amount collected from the consumers. The company is also involved in the business of reinsuring coverage under vehicle service contracts sold to consumers by dealers on vehicles financed by the company. It serves independent and franchised automobile dealers. The company was founded in 1972 and is headquartered in Southfield, Michigan.
company_profile.description_summary:
Finances vehicle purchases through independent and franchised automobile dealers across the United States. The company advances money to dealers in exchange for servicing rights to consumer loans, then purchases those loans and retains collections from consumers as its primary revenue source.

Additionally, the company reinsures vehicle service contracts sold by dealers on financed vehicles, diversifying its income streams beyond core lending operations.

We use net income DCF and Price-to-Book, which together capture both the earning power and the asset value of the loan book.

_notes.revenue_vs_net_income: This chart shows how much money the company brings in (revenue) versus how much it actually keeps as profit (net income) each year. A healthy company should show both lines growing over time. If revenue grows but net income shrinks, the company is spending more than it is earning — a warning sign worth investigating.
_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.
